title: pediocactus subterraneus
intro: Pediocactus subterraneus, also known as the desert poppy cactus or subterranean pincushion cactus, is a small, globular cactus species that grows underground in arid regions of the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Pediocactus subterraneus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
